title: Bulgaria exit polls: Former president Rumen Radev set to win parliamentary vote
content: Radev is unlikely to secure an outright majority in parliament. He is expected to fall short by approximately 10 - 12 seats, making coalition-building necessary.
snippet: Radev is unlikely to secure an outright majority in parliament. He is expected to fall short by approximately 10 - 12 seats, making coalition-building necessary.

title: Pope Leo XIV heads to shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade
content: Pope Leo XIV headed to a Catholic shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade. Earlier, the pontiff celebrated Mass before an estimated 100,000 people.
snippet: Pope Leo XIV headed to a Catholic shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade. Earlier, the pontiff celebrated Mass before an estimated 100,000 people.

title: Spain's Sánchez urges EU to break Association Agreement with Israel within 48 hours
content: During a Socialist Workers' Party rally in Gibraleón under the slogan 'Defend Public Services', on Sunday, Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Socialist candidate María Jesús Montero confirmed they would ask the EU to end its Association Agreement with Israel.

title: Ukraine targeted with over 230 Russian drones, Zelenskyy blasts US green light for Russian oil
content: Ukrainian Defence Forces targeted drone manufacturing plants in Russia and on occupied Ukrainian territory.
snippet: Ukrainian Defence Forces targeted drone manufacturing plants in Russia and on occupied Ukrainian territory.

title: Bulgaria votes in eighth election in five years
content: Bulgarians headed to the polls on Sunday in their eighth election in five years, with former president Rumen Radev's anti-corruption bloc leading in polls.
snippet: Bulgarians headed to the polls on Sunday in their eighth election in five years, with former president Rumen Radev's anti-corruption bloc leading in polls.

title: Exclusive: EU-based chemical producers ask Commission to probe Chinese group over deal in the UK
content: European chemical producers want the Commission to launch a probe into Chinese LB Group as it seeks to purchase a competitor’s plant in the UK to export to the EU, according to sources. The group fears it could be used to get around anti-dumping duties.

title: Spain's Sánchez builds anti-Trump coalition looking for political lifeline at home
content: Spanish PM led a progressive conference in Barcelona bringing together world leaders opposing MAGA politics, as Brazil's Lula lashed out at warlords and tech billionaires. "They're destroying democracy, workers and nature."

title: Europe's Patriots rally in Milan against immigration while Salvini backs Russian oil
content: Far-right leaders met in Milan calling for a plan to introduce remigration in Europe. League chief Salvini proposes a points-based residence permit and attacks the EU over its energy-crisis response, backing a reopening to Russian oil.

title: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
content: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

title: Ukraine: gunman opens fire in Kyiv, killing several people, mayor Vitali Klitschko said
content: A gunman who opened fire on people in Kyiv on Saturday, has left at least six dead and others wounded, the Ukrainian capital's mayor Vitali Klitschko said. According to police the man has now been shot dead after barricading himself in a nearby supermarket.

title: Trial of five men accused of sending exploding parcels for Russia starts in Lithuania
content: Investigators found that two test parcels had been sent to the United States and Canada, while two others intended for those countries were found in Amsterdam.
snippet: Investigators found that two test parcels had been sent to the United States and Canada, while two others intended for those countries were found in Amsterdam.

title: White House vs the pope: What is behind the clash and Catholic just war doctrine?
content: Theologians Euronews spoke to believe that the escalating war of words between Washington and the Holy See has raised important questions over Catholic and Christian moral thought.
snippet: Theologians Euronews spoke to believe that the escalating war of words between Washington and the Holy See has raised important questions over Catholic and Christian moral thought.

title: Oil flows from Russia via Druzhba pipeline to Hungary could resume next week, Magyar says
content: The pipeline, which delivers Russian oil to Hungary via Ukraine, has been a source of contention between Budapest and Kyiv after flows were stopped when it sustained damage in a drone strike.
snippet: The pipeline, which delivers Russian oil to Hungary via Ukraine, has been a source of contention between Budapest and Kyiv after flows were stopped when it sustained damage in a drone strike.

title: European leaders press ahead with 'defensive' mission after Iran reopens Hormuz
content: Meeting in Paris, the leaders said that a neutral mission may still be needed to safeguard shipping and prevent renewed disruption in the vital trade route.
snippet: Meeting in Paris, the leaders said that a neutral mission may still be needed to safeguard shipping and prevent renewed disruption in the vital trade route.

title: Venezuela and EU set out roadmap for renewed ties amid political thaw
content: Following talks in the Venezuelan parliament, Caracas and the EU have agreed on a new cooperation agenda. The shift aligns with a broader economic pivot, as the nation resumes ties with the IMF and the World Bank.

title: Spain immigration officers cancel strike after deal to ease workload from mass migrant amnesty
content: Spain has granted amnesty to immigrants who are in the country illegally before, doing so six times between 1986 and 2005.
snippet: Spain has granted amnesty to immigrants who are in the country illegally before, doing so six times between 1986 and 2005.

title: German rescuers plan to use air cushions in bid to save Timmy the stranded whale
content: The rescue attempt, proposed by two entrepreneurs, involves lifting the whale using inflatable cushions and transporting it with pontoons.
snippet: The rescue attempt, proposed by two entrepreneurs, involves lifting the whale using inflatable cushions and transporting it with pontoons.

title: Pope Leo XIV slams 'tyrants' ransacking world on high-security visit to Cameroon
content: On arrival in the country on Wednesday, the pope appealed to Cameroon's leaders to examine their "conscience" and tackle corruption and rights abuses.
snippet: On arrival in the country on Wednesday, the pope appealed to Cameroon's leaders to examine their "conscience" and tackle corruption and rights abuses.

title: Paris court jails ex-Élysée silverware keeper for porcelain theft ring
content: A former presidential tableware steward was found guilty of stealing the pieces, his partner of helping sell them online, and a Louvre employee of buying some of the items.
snippet: A former presidential tableware steward was found guilty of stealing the pieces, his partner of helping sell them online, and a Louvre employee of buying some of the items.

content: Civil Defence Minister Carl-Oskar Bohlin said the aim of the operation was to disrupt the functioning of a facility that supplies heating.
snippet: Civil Defence Minister Carl-Oskar Bohlin said the aim of the operation was to disrupt the functioning of a facility that supplies heating.

title: EU and Magyar agreed to work together for release of EU cash after weekend talks
content: Ursula von der Leyen's right-hand man sent to Budapest to begin work for the release of €10 billion as Magyar races to unfreeze the cash in exchange of reforms. According to sources involved in the negotiations, the release of funds is not connected to Ukraine.

title: Moscow bombards Ukraine while Kyiv targets Russian oil facilities
content: Ukraine struck multiple Russian oil refineries with drones on Saturday while simultaneously repelling a 219-drone Russian assault on its own territory.
snippet: Ukraine struck multiple Russian oil refineries with drones on Saturday while simultaneously repelling a 219-drone Russian assault on its own territory.

title: Deep inside the heart: the innovative catheter designed to revolutionise the treatment of arrhythmia
content: Clinical studies are underway to test an innovative catheter that’s able to accurately and safely heal unhealthy heart tissue with single, shorter energy pulses. A technological breakthrough designed, manufactured and tested in the European Union.

title: EU downplays jet fuel shortage risks despite IEA warning
content: EU officials and the International Energy Agency have contrasting narratives over jet fuel supply. While the European commission minimises the risk, it acknowledges that the market remains “tight” and says the EU is prepared to respond appropriately.

title: Rights groups in Sweden slam government 'honest living' proposal for migrants
content: The Swedish Refugee Law Centre, an organisation that provides legal assistance to asylum seekers, says the proposals will make the process for residence permits unpredictable.
snippet: The Swedish Refugee Law Centre, an organisation that provides legal assistance to asylum seekers, says the proposals will make the process for residence permits unpredictable.

title: European far-right faces uncertainty after Orbán’s setback ahead of Milan rally
content: The European far-right Patriots for Europe are facing uncertainty after Viktor Orbán’s electoral setback in Hungary raised questions about his future role in the party he helped create. A rally in Milan on Saturday will serve as an early test of the party's cohesion and political momentum.

title: Did Apple remove Lebanon’s villages from its maps? It says they were never there
content: Claims that the US tech giant, Apple, intentionally erased settlement names in southern Lebanon recently went viral. The tech giant says they were never included.
snippet: Claims that the US tech giant, Apple, intentionally erased settlement names in southern Lebanon recently went viral. The tech giant says they were never included.

title: Instagram, TikTok and Facebook: Which European countries want to ban social media for minors?

snippet: After Australia banned social media for under-16s in December, European nations are now debating their own restrictions as Brussels mulls a "European solution".

title: Four years of Gepard in Ukraine: How the vintage German weapon is proving its worth
content: Four years after Berlin's decision to deliver it, it has become clear that the once decommissioned Gepard tank is now a key system in Ukraine's air defence — and one of the most effective "drone killers" in the fight against Russia.

title: Watch: Sunday elections — Bulgaria's fight for stable government?
content: Bulgaria heads to the polls this Sunday for its eighth parliamentary election in five years. The country is the poorest in the EU according to GDP per capita, and the electorate is exhausted by a prolonged political crisis. So, who is stepping in to fill the void?

title: Europe-led coalition prepares mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
content: A group of more than 40 countries is meeting in Paris to finalise early plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The France and UK-led coalition insists the operation will only kick in when the war is over. Countries say they are ready to send warships, personnel and demining support.

title: 'Ripple effects of Iran war could lead to other conflicts,' UN development chief tells Euronews
content: The head of the UN Development Programme told Euronews that the impact of the Middle East conflict is being felt "way beyond the region", and that, in the absence of a resolution, the groundwork for further conflicts is being laid.

title: Hungary shows limits of 'anti-EU narrative' pushed by far-right, Ribera tells Euronews
content: Hungary’s election, which saw Viktor Orbán ousted after 16 years in power, signals that anti-European narrative pushed by the far-right has hit a wall, European Commission Executive Vice-President Ribera told Euronews.

title: No country can legally restrict navigation in Strait of Hormuz, UN maritime chief says
content: The Secretary-General of the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO) told Euronews that shipping shouldn’t become "collateral" in conflicts.

title: Iran to blame for closing Strait of Hormuz, says Gulf leader
content: The Gulf Chief Jasem Mohamed AlBudaiwi told Euronews that Iran is responsible for weaponising the Strait to score a geopolitical win and should deal with the effects.
snippet: The Gulf Chief Jasem Mohamed AlBudaiwi told Euronews that Iran is responsible for weaponising the Strait to score a geopolitical win and should deal with the effects.

title: EU rushes to Budapest talks with Magyar team to unlock frozen funds amid Ukraine tensions
content: European Commission officials are due to meet the team of Hungary’s prime minister-designate, Péter Magyar, in Budapest on Friday, just five days after his election victory, to begin the process of unfreezing €17 billion in EU funds, with Ukraine-related disputes also on the agenda.

title: Bulgaria prepares for disinformation ahead of snap elections
content: Disinformation researchers warn that Bulgaria has "one of the most permissive information environments" for non-democratic malign manipulation within Europe.
snippet: Disinformation researchers warn that Bulgaria has "one of the most permissive information environments" for non-democratic malign manipulation within Europe.

title: Is it worth stockpiling cash as phsyical currency makes comeback in Poland?
content: While the trend towards the complete digitalisation of payments prevailed not long ago, today central banks and governments say that physical money may prove indispensable in crisis situations.

title: Orbán's defeat and US shift raise hopes to unblock EU sanctions on Russia

title: EU to slash power bills by cutting taxes and grid costs

title: Berlin approves defence initiatives as domestic industry receives drone and frigate deals
content: New combat drones are planned for use by the so-called Lithuania Brigade, a unit of around 5,000 soldiers who guarantee the defence and security of NATO's eastern flank until the end of 2027.

title: Where is Szijjártó? Hungarian minister to skip EU meeting after vanishing from public view

title: Berlin offers free entry for litter picking to make tourists do their bit
content: Visitors to Berlin could receive vouchers this summer for picking up litter. The BerlinPay scheme takes its cue from Copenhagen, linking green behaviour with enjoyable rewards.
snippet: Visitors to Berlin could receive vouchers this summer for picking up litter. The BerlinPay scheme takes its cue from Copenhagen, linking green behaviour with enjoyable rewards.

title: Russia hits Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro in one of deadliest attacks on Ukraine
content: Russia unleashed a massive drone and missile barrage on major cities across Ukraine, killing at least 16 and injuring scores, as Zelenskyy urged more support from allies.
snippet: Russia unleashed a massive drone and missile barrage on major cities across Ukraine, killing at least 16 and injuring scores, as Zelenskyy urged more support from allies.
